metadata.description: Unified registry: explorer/timelines (landscape UI) + researchers/institutions/standards/legislation (structured entities). Cross-references QIF-RESEARCH-SOURCES.md.

explorer.name: Columbia University
explorer.subtitle: NeuroRights Foundation
explorer.contribution: Five neurorights, Chile legislation, Nature call-to-action

explorer.name: Stanford University
explorer.subtitle: Center for Law & Biosciences
explorer.contribution: Neurolaw, brain data spectrum, legal scholarship

explorer.name: UPenn
explorer.subtitle: Center for Neuroscience & Society
explorer.contribution: Neuroethics toolbox, cosmetic neurology, neuromodesty

explorer.name: Duke University
explorer.subtitle: Science & Society
explorer.contribution: Cognitive liberty, Battle for Your Brain, incriminating thoughts

explorer.name: Johns Hopkins
explorer.subtitle: Berman Institute / CELLS
explorer.contribution: Three-tiered governance, device abandonment, NAM framework

explorer.name: ETH Zurich
explorer.subtitle: Health Ethics & Policy Lab
explorer.contribution: Neurorights taxonomy, brain data governance, anti-inflationism

explorer.name: Oxford University
explorer.subtitle: Uehiro Centre
explorer.contribution: Enhancement ethics, cognitive justice, philosophical foundations

explorer.name: UBC
explorer.subtitle: National Core for Neuroethics
explorer.contribution: Neuroethics Canada. Neuroimaging ethics, DBS identity, Indigenous neuroscience ethics

explorer.name: Georgia State
explorer.subtitle: Center for Neuroimaging & Neuroethics
explorer.contribution: Only US PhD concentration in neuroethics. Global Neuroethics Summit co-founder

explorer.name: UCSF
explorer.subtitle: Decision Lab / BRAIN Neuroethics
explorer.contribution: NIH BRAIN-funded neuroethics. DBS decision-making, dementia autonomy, clinical BCI ethics

explorer.name: Penn State
explorer.subtitle: Neuroethics & Society
explorer.contribution: IEEE P7700 Neurotechnology chair. Public attitudes toward neurotech, neurostimulation ethics

explorer.name: INSERM / Sorbonne
explorer.subtitle: Neuroscience Paris-Seine
explorer.contribution: Co-chaired UNESCO Ad Hoc Expert Group producing first global neurotech ethics framework (2025)

explorer.name: RHUNE Network
explorer.subtitle: German National Hub
explorer.contribution: Responsible Use of Neurotechnologies for Europe. BMBF-funded, bridges Freiburg, Tübingen, Berlin

explorer.name: Baylor College of Medicine
explorer.subtitle: Center for Medical Ethics
explorer.contribution: Neural data consent, genomic/neural data ethics intersection, clinical neuroethics

explorer.name: UNESCO
explorer.subtitle: International Bioethics Committee
explorer.contribution: First global neurotech ethics recommendation (2025)

explorer.name: OECD
explorer.subtitle: Neurotechnology Policy
explorer.contribution: Nine-principle innovation governance (2019)

explorer.name: WHO
explorer.subtitle: Ethics & Governance
explorer.contribution: First neurotechnology guidance document (2025)

explorer.name: Council of Europe
explorer.subtitle: Bioethics Committee
explorer.contribution: Strategic Action Plan on Neurotechnology (2025)

explorer.name: Int'l Neuroethics Society
explorer.subtitle: Professional Society
explorer.contribution: First professional society for neuroethics. Founded at Asilomar by Gazzaniga, Farah, Illes, Wolpe

explorer.name: NeuroRights Foundation
explorer.subtitle: Columbia University
explorer.contribution: Policy vehicle driving Chile, California, Colorado legislation. 2025 consumer neurotech data audit

explorer.name: Chile Constitution (2021)
explorer.subtitle: First neurorights law
explorer.contribution: First country to constitutionally protect neurorights (mental integrity)

explorer.name: Colorado / California / Montana / Minnesota
explorer.subtitle: US State Neural Data Laws (2024)
explorer.contribution: Four U.S. states enact neural data protections in 2024

explorer.name: UN Human Rights Council
explorer.subtitle: Neurotechnology Mandate
explorer.contribution: Final report on neurotechnology and human rights (September 2024)

explorer.name: Latin American Parliament
explorer.subtitle: Model Law on Neurorights (2023)
explorer.contribution: Regional model law for harmonized neurorights legislation across Latin America

explorer.name: Global Privacy Assembly
explorer.subtitle: Neural Data Resolution
explorer.contribution: Resolution on principles for neural data processing and cognitive freedom

explorer.name: Tadayoshi Kohno
explorer.subtitle: University of Washington
explorer.contribution: Co-author of the foundational neurosecurity paper (Denning, Matsuoka, Kohno 2009). Threat model for implantable neural devices.

explorer.name: Tamara Denning
explorer.subtitle: University of Washington
explorer.contribution: Lead author on the first neurosecurity paper. Defined attack surfaces for implantable BMIs.

explorer.name: Ivan Martinovic
explorer.subtitle: University of Oxford
explorer.contribution: First empirical BCI side-channel attack. P300-based extraction of PINs and locations from consumer EEG.

explorer.name: Dawn Song
explorer.subtitle: UC Berkeley
explorer.contribution: Co-authored the landmark BCI side-channel paper. MacArthur Fellow, one of the most-cited in computer security.

explorer.name: Laurie Pycroft
explorer.subtitle: University of Oxford
explorer.contribution: Coined and systematized "brainjacking" for implanted neurostimulators.

explorer.name: Tamara Bonaci
explorer.subtitle: Northeastern / UW
explorer.contribution: BCI game attack vectors. Subliminal stimuli can extract private information during BCI use.

explorer.name: Howard Chizeck
explorer.subtitle: University of Washington
explorer.contribution: Neural engineering security. BCI privacy through signal obfuscation and access control.

explorer.name: Kevin Fu
explorer.subtitle: Northeastern University
explorer.detail: Archimedes Center for Medical Device Security
explorer.contribution: Medical device cybersecurity pioneer. Former FDA Acting Director of Medical Device Cybersecurity.

explorer.name: Nitesh Saxena
explorer.subtitle: Texas A&M University
explorer.contribution: BCI authentication protocols. P300 brainwave attacks and neural biometric spoofing.

explorer.name: Marcello Ienca
explorer.subtitle: TU Munich / ETH Zurich
explorer.contribution: Brain data governance at the security-ethics intersection. Neurorights taxonomy and anti-inflationism.

explorer.name: Sergio Lopez Bernal
explorer.subtitle: University of Murcia
explorer.contribution: BCI cybersecurity survey and neural cyberattack taxonomy. "Eight Reasons" why BCIs are not secure.

explorer.name: Tyler Schroder
explorer.subtitle: Yale Digital Ethics Center
explorer.contribution: Detailed 2025 BCI cyber risk assessment. Measures for safeguarding brain implants.

explorer.name: Qiben Yan
explorer.subtitle: Michigan State University
explorer.contribution: Sensor injection attacks (acoustic, ultrasonic). SEIT lab. No verified BCI-specific neural publications — primary work targets voice assistants and IMU sensors.

explorer.name: Dongrui Wu
explorer.subtitle: HUST / Zhejiang Lab
explorer.contribution: Most prolific BCI security ML group. Backdoor attacks, evasion attacks, federated defense.

explorer.name: Bao-Liang Lu
explorer.subtitle: Shanghai Jiao Tong University
explorer.contribution: Professor X: invisible backdoor attack on EEG BCI via clean-label poisoning.

explorer.name: Wanzeng Kong
explorer.subtitle: Hangzhou Dianzi University
explorer.contribution: Brainprint adversarial attacks. Time-frequency perturbations that defeat EEG biometric authentication.

explorer.name: Xinyu Jiang
explorer.subtitle: Fudan University (co-authors at Penn State, Imperial)
explorer.contribution: First systematic cybersecurity survey across both central and peripheral neural interfaces.

explorer.name: University of Washington
explorer.subtitle: Security & Privacy Research Lab
explorer.contribution: Founded the neurosecurity field (2009). Implantable device threat modeling, BCI game attacks, neural signal privacy.

explorer.name: University of Oxford
explorer.subtitle: Functional Neurosurgery + InfoSec
explorer.contribution: Brainjacking threat models for implanted DBS devices. EEG side-channel attacks on consumer BCIs.

explorer.name: Northeastern University
explorer.subtitle: Archimedes Center for Medical Device Security
explorer.contribution: Medical device cybersecurity pioneer. $22M total research. Neural implant security under $3.5M NSF grant.

explorer.name: Yale University
explorer.subtitle: Digital Ethics Center
explorer.contribution: Detailed BCI cyber risk assessment (2025). Interdisciplinary cybersecurity-neuroethics approach.

explorer.name: UC Berkeley
explorer.subtitle: Computer Security Research
explorer.contribution: Co-produced the first empirical BCI side-channel attack paper (USENIX 2012).

explorer.name: TU Munich / ETH Zurich
explorer.subtitle: Ethics of AI and Neuroscience
explorer.contribution: Brain data governance, neurorights and security intersection, state-of-the-art BCI security survey (ACM 2021).

explorer.name: University of Murcia
explorer.subtitle: BCI Cybersecurity Group
explorer.contribution: Neural cyberattack taxonomy. Highest-volume BCI security publication group.

explorer.name: Graz University of Technology
explorer.subtitle: Institute of Neural Engineering
explorer.contribution: BCI conference venue and neural engineering research. EEG-based authentication and biosignal processing.

explorer.name: Michigan State University
explorer.subtitle: SEIT Lab
explorer.contribution: Sensor injection attacks (acoustic, ultrasonic, EMI). No verified BCI-specific neural publications.

explorer.name: Texas A&M University
explorer.subtitle: SPIES Lab / Global Cyber Research Institute
explorer.contribution: BCI authentication, neural biometric security, brainwave spoofing.

explorer.name: Brown University
explorer.subtitle: BrainGate Consortium
explorer.contribution: Intracortical BCI clinical trials. First long-term human implant data on failure modes.

explorer.name: HUST (Wuhan)
explorer.subtitle: AI & Automation / MoE Key Lab
explorer.contribution: Most prolific BCI ML-security group globally. Backdoor, evasion, federated defense.

explorer.name: Shanghai Jiao Tong University
explorer.subtitle: BCMI Lab
explorer.contribution: Professor X invisible backdoor attack. SEED emotion dataset (most-used EEG dataset).

explorer.name: Fudan University
explorer.subtitle: Multi-institution Survey Group
explorer.contribution: First cybersecurity survey covering both CNS and PNS neural interfaces.

explorer.name: Zhejiang University
explorer.subtitle: Qiushi Academy / BCI Group
explorer.contribution: China's first human BCI implant (2020). Hardware-software security intersection.

explorer.name: IEEE
explorer.subtitle: BMI Standards Roadmap
explorer.contribution: Identified BCI-specific security standards as an open standardization gap. IEEE 2794 (brain data).

explorer.name: NIST
explorer.subtitle: Cybersecurity Framework
explorer.contribution: Provides the security control baseline. No neurotech-specific profile exists yet.

explorer.name: FDA
explorer.subtitle: Premarket Cybersecurity Guidance
explorer.contribution: Requires threat modeling for connected medical devices. No neural-specific requirements.

explorer.name: ISO/IEC
explorer.subtitle: Medical Device Security
explorer.contribution: Implant standards and industrial security. No BCI-specific standard exists.

explorer.name: MITRE
explorer.subtitle: ATT&CK / CWE / CVE
explorer.contribution: Attack taxonomy and vulnerability classification. No neural device attack techniques catalogued.

explorer.name: CISA
explorer.subtitle: Medical Device Advisories
explorer.contribution: Issues advisories for connected device vulnerabilities. Has not addressed neural implants.

explorer.name: EU AI Act
explorer.subtitle: High-Risk AI Classification
explorer.contribution: Classifies certain neurotech as high-risk AI systems. Full application August 2026.

explorer.name: FIRST / CVSS SIG
explorer.subtitle: Vulnerability Scoring
explorer.contribution: Scores IT vulnerabilities. No neural impact metrics exist in the standard.

explorer.name: China MoST
explorer.subtitle: BCI Ethics Guidelines
explorer.contribution: First Chinese government-issued ethics guidelines for neural devices. Not binding regulation.

explorer.name: Spain
explorer.subtitle: Carta de Derechos Digitales (2021)
explorer.contribution: First European nation to include neurorights in a digital rights charter

explorer.name: Brazil
explorer.subtitle: PEC 29/2023
explorer.contribution: Proposes adding mental integrity and algorithmic transparency to fundamental rights

explorer.name: Mexico
explorer.subtitle: GLNN (2024)
explorer.contribution: Most comprehensive national neurorights legislation proposed (92 articles + 35 amendments)

explorer.name: EU Parliament STOA
explorer.subtitle: Mental Privacy Study (2024)
explorer.contribution: Study on protection of mental privacy in neuroscience

explorer.name: European Brain Council
explorer.subtitle: Charter (2025)
explorer.contribution: Pan-European responsible development charter for neurotechnologies

explorer.name: France
explorer.subtitle: National Charter (2023)
explorer.contribution: French charter implementing OECD 2019 neurotechnology recommendation

explorer.name: QIF / Qinnovate
explorer.subtitle: Open Neural Atlas
explorer.contribution: Proposed open security framework. NISS, TARA, Coherence Metric, NSP.

neuroethics.label: Fritz Jahr coins "Bio-Ethik"
neuroethics.desc: First use of the term in Kosmos journal, proposing a bioethical imperative extending moral consideration to all living things

neuroethics.label: Nuremberg Code
neuroethics.desc: First international ethics code for human experimentation, born from the Doctors' Trial

neuroethics.label: Declaration of Helsinki
neuroethics.desc: World Medical Association establishes ethical principles for medical research on humans

neuroethics.label: Potter revives "Bioethics"
neuroethics.desc: Van Rensselaer Potter publishes "Bioethics: The Science of Survival" in Perspectives in Biology and Medicine

neuroethics.label: National Research Act
neuroethics.desc: U.S. Congress creates the National Commission for the Protection of Human Subjects in response to the Tuskegee study

neuroethics.label: Belmont Report
neuroethics.desc: Three core principles for human subjects research: respect for persons, beneficence, justice

neuroethics.label: Beauchamp & Childress
neuroethics.desc: Principles of Biomedical Ethics: autonomy, beneficence, non-maleficence, justice

neuroethics.label: "Neuroethics" defined
neuroethics.desc: William Safire's keynote at the Dana Foundation conference establishes the modern field

neuroethics.label: Brain Overclaim Syndrome
neuroethics.desc: Stephen Morse warns neuroscience claims are routinely overclaimed when applied to law. "Neuromodesty" follows in 2011

neuroethics.label: Int'l Neuroethics Society
neuroethics.desc: Founded at Asilomar by Gazzaniga, Farah, Illes, and Wolpe

neuroethics.label: Neurorights
neuroethics.desc: Ienca & Andorno and Yuste et al. (Nature) independently propose neurorights

neuroethics.label: OECD Recommendation
neuroethics.desc: Nine-principle governance for responsible innovation in neurotechnology

neuroethics.label: Chile Constitution
neuroethics.desc: First country to constitutionally protect neurorights (mental integrity)

neuroethics.label: Latin American Model Law
neuroethics.desc: Regional model law for harmonized neurorights legislation

neuroethics.label: US State Neural Data Laws
neuroethics.desc: Colorado, California, Montana, Minnesota enact neural data protections

neuroethics.label: China BCI Ethics + UN HRC
neuroethics.desc: China MoST BCI ethics guidelines. UN HRC advisory on neurotechnology. Global Privacy Assembly neural data resolution

neuroethics.label: UNESCO + WHO + MIND Act
neuroethics.desc: UNESCO global neurotech ethics framework (194 states). WHO neurotechnology guidance. US MIND Act

neurosecurity.label: "Neurosecurity" coined
neurosecurity.authors: Denning, T., Matsuoka, Y., Kohno, T.
neurosecurity.desc: First formal definition of neurosecurity; wireless hijacking, malicious reprogramming, eavesdropping as threat classes.

neurosecurity.label: EEG side-channel — PIN extraction
neurosecurity.authors: Martinovic, I., Davies, D., Frank, M., Perito, D., Ros, T., Song, D.
neurosecurity.desc: Consumer EEG headsets extract PINs and bank info via P300 responses to subliminal financial stimuli.

neurosecurity.label: Subliminal stimulus — brain malware
neurosecurity.authors: Bonaci, T., Calo, R., Chizeck, H.
neurosecurity.desc: Malicious BCI app disguised as game harvests P300 responses to extract PINs, credit card numbers without consent.

neurosecurity.label: Securing the Exocortex
neurosecurity.authors: Bonaci, T., Herron, J.A., Matlack, C., Chizeck, H.J.
neurosecurity.desc: BCI as external cognitive layer. Security challenges for brain-computer symbiosis.

neurosecurity.label: Early BCI security taxonomy
neurosecurity.authors: Li, Q., Ding, D., Conti, M.
neurosecurity.desc: One of the first IEEE conference papers taxonomizing BCI security and privacy challenges.

neurosecurity.label: Firewall for Brain — privacy BCI
neurosecurity.authors: Takabi, H.
neurosecurity.desc: First 'firewall for brain' concept in published literature. Privacy-preserving BCI ecosystem.

neurosecurity.label: Brainjacking — implant takeover
neurosecurity.authors: Pycroft, L., Boccard, S.G., Owen, S.L.F., Stein, J.F., Fitzgerald, J.J., Green, A.L., Aziz, T.Z.
neurosecurity.desc: Threat taxonomy for DBS implants: therapy cessation, battery drain, motor impairment, mood alteration, pain induction.

neurosecurity.label: BCI attack taxonomy
neurosecurity.authors: Ienca, M., Haselager, P.
neurosecurity.desc: Four attack classes: neural data theft, unauthorized manipulation, privacy violation, brain-hacking.

neurosecurity.label: Subliminal probing — sub-13ms stimulus
neurosecurity.authors: Frank, M., Hwu, T., Jain, S., Knight, R.T., Martinovic, I., Mittal, P., Perito, D., Sluganovic, I., Song, D.
neurosecurity.desc: Subliminal EEG probing below conscious perception threshold (13.3ms) infers private information from 27 participants.

neurosecurity.label: Wireless neurostimulator protocol attack
neurosecurity.authors: Marin, E., Singelée, D., Yang, B., Volskiy, V., Vandenbosch, G., Nuttin, B., Preneel, B.
neurosecurity.desc: Reverse-engineered unencrypted RF protocol of commercial neurostimulator; software-radio attacks alter stimulation parameters.

neurosecurity.label: Adversarial ML — EEG CNN attack (first)
neurosecurity.authors: Zhang, X., Wu, D.
neurosecurity.desc: First study showing CNN classifiers in EEG-BCIs vulnerable to adversarial perturbations; cross-model transferability enables black-box attacks.

neurosecurity.label: Neuronal flooding/scanning — miniature implants
neurosecurity.authors: López Bernal, S., Huertas Celdrán, A., Fernandez Maimo, L., Barros, M.T., Balasubramaniam, S., Martínez Pérez, G.
neurosecurity.desc: Introduces FLO and SCA attack primitives on micron-scale BCI implants. Parent paper for neural cyberattack simulation line.

neurosecurity.label: EEG biometric spoofing — smartwatch correlation
neurosecurity.authors: Shukla, D., Kundu, P.P., Malapati, R., Poudel, S., Jin, Z., Phoha, V.V.
neurosecurity.desc: EEG authentication bypassed using only smartwatch motion data correlated to victim hand movements.

neurosecurity.label: Neuronal jamming — invasive BCI
neurosecurity.authors: López Bernal, S., Huertas Celdrán, A., Martínez Pérez, G.
neurosecurity.desc: Invasive BCI attack prevents neuronal spike generation via jamming through compromised electrodes.

neurosecurity.label: P300 signal spoofing — noise injection
neurosecurity.authors: Martínez Beltrán, E.T., Quiles Pérez, M., López Bernal, S., Huertas Celdrán, A., Martínez Pérez, G.
neurosecurity.desc: Four noise-based attacks generate fake P300 waves; 74% classification degradation in processing phase.

neurosecurity.label: SSVEP signal injection — square wave
neurosecurity.authors: Bian, R., Meng, L., Wu, D.
neurosecurity.desc: Simple square waves fool CCA classifiers in SSVEP-based BCIs, requiring no model knowledge.

neurosecurity.label: Backdoor / data poisoning — EEG classifier
neurosecurity.authors: Meng, L., Jiang, X., Huang, J., Zeng, Z., Yu, S., Jung, T-P., Lin, C-T., Chavarriaga, R., Wu, D.
neurosecurity.desc: Narrow-period-pulse poisoning embeds unsynchronized backdoor trigger in EEG training data.

neurosecurity.label: Eight neural cyberattack classes
neurosecurity.authors: López Bernal, S., Huertas Celdrán, A., Martínez Pérez, G.
neurosecurity.desc: Eight archetypes: Flooding, Jamming, Scanning, Selective Forwarding, Spoofing, Sybil, Sinkhole, Nonce.

neurosecurity.label: RF physical-layer EEG takeover
neurosecurity.authors: Armengol-Urpi, A., Kovacs, R., Sarma, S.E.
neurosecurity.desc: Remote RF injection hijacks virtual keyboard speller, drone controller, and meditation app across three device classes.

neurosecurity.label: Professor X invisible backdoor
neurosecurity.authors: Liu, Song, He, Lu, Zheng
neurosecurity.desc: Clean-label poisoning on EEG BCI using RL-selected electrode injection. Invisible to inspection.

neurosecurity.label: Brainprint adversarial attack
neurosecurity.authors: Yi, Ming, Liu, Kong
neurosecurity.desc: Time-frequency perturbations defeat EEG biometric authentication systems.

neurosecurity.label: Comprehensive BCI risk assessment
neurosecurity.authors: Schroder et al.
neurosecurity.desc: Detailed BCI cyber risk assessment covering multiple attack vectors and mitigations.

neurosecurity.label: QIF
neurosecurity.desc: First open technical security specification for neural interfaces (in development)

scholarship.title: Towards New Human Rights in the Age of Neuroscience and Neurotechnology
scholarship.desc: Introduces cognitive liberty, mental privacy, mental integrity, psychological continuity as neurorights.

scholarship.authors: Yuste, R., Goering, S. et al.
scholarship.title: Four ethical priorities for neurotechnologies and AI
scholarship.desc: Call-to-action for neurorights protections. Basis for Columbia NeuroRights Foundation.

scholarship.title: Hacking the Brain: Brain-Computer Interfacing Technology and the Ethics of Neurosecurity
scholarship.desc: First systematic treatment of BCI attack vectors within an ethics framework.

scholarship.title: Towards a Governance Framework for Brain Data
scholarship.desc: Four regulatory areas for brain data: binding regulation, ethics/soft law, responsible innovation, human rights.

scholarship.title: Cyberneurosecurity
scholarship.desc: Coined 'cyberneurosecurity' — ethical/policy issues from internet-enabled BCIs including neural manipulation and self-hacking.

scholarship.title: Ethical Governance Strategies for the Responsible Innovation of Neurotechnologies: A Scoping Review
scholarship.desc: Scoping review of 51 articles identifying six governance strategies for neurotechnology.

scholarship.title: Neurotechnology Governance in the United States: Gaps and Opportunities
scholarship.desc: Reviews Colorado and California neural data rights legislation.

scholarship.title: Standardization of Neurotechnology for Brain-Machine Interfacing: State of the Art and Recommendations
scholarship.desc: IEEE-affiliated review noting scarcity of mandatory BCI security standards.

scholarship.title: Decoding mental states from brain activity in humans
scholarship.desc: Foundational review of multivariate pattern analysis for neural decoding. Identified ethical issues with mental privacy.

scholarship.title: Big Brain Data: On the Responsible Use of Brain Data from Clinical and Consumer-Directed Neurotechnological Devices
scholarship.desc: Brain data governance framework. Argued for treating brain data as a special protected category.

scholarship.title: Neurorights: A Human Rights-Based Approach for Governing Neurotechnologies
scholarship.venue: Cambridge Handbook of Responsible AI, Ch. 24
scholarship.desc: Conceptual underspecification of mental privacy/integrity. Key QIF rigor check source.

scholarship.title: Novel Neurorights: From Nonsense to Substance
scholarship.desc: Anti-inflationism: proposed consolidating neurorights into mental self-determination.

scholarship.title: Oversight of Direct-to-Consumer Neurotechnologies
scholarship.desc: Policy commentary on oversight gaps for consumer neurotech. Previously misattributed to DOI 10.1007/s11948-020-00218-0 (which is Rainey et al.). Corrected 2026-03-05.

scholarship.title: Rationales and Approaches to Protecting Brain Data: A Scoping Review
scholarship.desc: Scoping review mapping gaps between existing brain data frameworks and emerging AI risks.

scholarship.title: Adversarial Attacks and Defenses in Physiological Computing: A Systematic Review
scholarship.desc: Systematic review of adversarial attacks across EEG, EMG, ECG, eye tracking modalities.

scholarship.authors: Cornejo-Plaza, M.I., Cippitani, R., Pasquino, V.
scholarship.title: Chilean Supreme Court Ruling on the Protection of Brain Activity
scholarship.desc: First Supreme Court neurorights ruling analysis. Landmark judicial interpretation of Chile's constitutional neurorights.

scholarship.title: Challenges of the Inconsistent Neurorights Framework in Latin America
scholarship.desc: Critique of inconsistencies across Latin American neurorights implementations.

scholarship.title: Cultural Diversity is Crucial for African Neuroethics
scholarship.desc: Ubuntu philosophy applied to neuroethics. Non-Western perspective.

scholarship.title: Is the European Data Protection Regulation Sufficient for Neurotechnology?
scholarship.desc: GDPR adequacy analysis for brain data. Identifies EU data protection gaps.

scholarship.title: The New Regulation of Non-Medical Neurotechnologies in the EU
scholarship.desc: EU regulatory gap analysis for non-medical neurotech.

scholarship.title: What an International Declaration on Neurotechnologies Could Look Like
scholarship.desc: 25 desiderata for UNESCO neurotechnology declaration.

scholarship.title: Neurorights: Do We Need New Human Rights? Freedom of Thought Reconsideration
scholarship.desc: Argues existing ICCPR Art. 18 may cover neurorights without new instruments.

scholarship.authors: Wei, B., Cheng, S., Feng, Y.
scholarship.title: Neural Personal Information and Its Legal Protection: Evidence from China
scholarship.desc: Chinese neural data legal protection analysis.

researchers.name: Tadayoshi Kohno
researchers.qifRelevance: Coined 'neurosecurity' in 2009. Foundational BCI security research.

researchers.name: Ivan Martinovic
researchers.qifRelevance: First EEG side-channel attack demonstration (USENIX 2012).

researchers.name: Tamara Bonaci
researchers.qifRelevance: BCI app store privacy model. Subliminal image attack on BCI.

researchers.name: Marcello Ienca
researchers.qifRelevance: Neurorights pioneer. Co-authored foundational neurorights paper (2017). BCI security lifecycle survey.

researchers.name: Rafael Yuste
researchers.qifRelevance: Four ethical priorities for neurotech (Nature 2017). Neurorights Foundation founder. Chile neurorights advisor.

researchers.name: Nita Farahany
researchers.qifRelevance: Battle for Your Brain (2023). Leading voice on cognitive liberty and neural data rights.

researchers.name: Sara Goering
researchers.qifRelevance: IEEE Neuroethics Framework contributor. Responsible BCI development guidelines.

researchers.name: Gabriel Lazaro-Munoz
researchers.qifRelevance: DBS post-trial access ethics. Device abandonment concerns.

researchers.name: Lubin Meng
researchers.institution: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
researchers.qifRelevance: EEG adversarial robustness benchmark. Systematic adversarial ML applied to BCI.

researchers.name: Tyler Schroder
researchers.qifRelevance: Most comprehensive BCI security paper (2025). Neuroethics journal publication.

researchers.name: Deborah J.H. Mathews
researchers.institution: Johns Hopkins Berman Institute of Bioethics
researchers.qifRelevance: Three-tiered governance framework for emerging tech. QIF as Tier 3 enforcement tool.

researchers.name: Juan Lopez Bernal
researchers.qifRelevance: BCI security taxonomy papers. 4 TARA techniques derived from taxonomy gap analysis.

researchers.name: Song Liu
researchers.qifRelevance: Professor X invisible backdoor attack on EEG BCI (2024). Novel invisible trigger mechanism.

researchers.name: Yanjie Kong
researchers.qifRelevance: Brainprint adversarial attacks (ICME 2024). EEG biometric spoofing.

researchers.name: Xue Jiang
researchers.institution: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
researchers.qifRelevance: CNS+PNS neural interface cybersecurity survey (2023). Comprehensive threat landscape.

researchers.name: Judy Illes
researchers.qifRelevance: UBC National Core for Neuroethics director. Pioneer in neuroimaging ethics and Indigenous neuroscience ethics.

researchers.name: Karen Rommelfanger
researchers.qifRelevance: Only US PhD concentration in neuroethics. Center for Neuroimaging and Neuroethics director.

researchers.name: Winston Chiong
researchers.qifRelevance: UCSF Decision Lab. NIH BRAIN Initiative neuroethics. DBS informed consent research.

researchers.name: Laura Cabrera
researchers.qifRelevance: IEEE P7700 Neurotechnology Standards chair. Public attitudes toward neurotechnology research.

researchers.name: Herve Chneiweiss
researchers.qifRelevance: Co-chair of UNESCO Ad Hoc Expert Group on Neurotechnology (2025). French neuroscience policy leadership.

researchers.name: Amy McGuire
researchers.qifRelevance: Neural data consent frameworks. Genomic-neural ethics intersection.

researchers.name: Georg Kellmeyer
researchers.qifRelevance: Mental privacy and integrity conceptual analysis. Highlighted underspecification of core neuroethics concepts.

researchers.name: Christoph Bublitz
researchers.qifRelevance: Mental freedom and cognitive liberty framework. Anti-inflationism perspective on neurorights.

researchers.name: Eric Racine
researchers.qifRelevance: Neuro-realism, neuro-essentialism, neuro-policy triad (2005). Foundational neuroethics rigor.

researchers.name: Russell Poldrack
researchers.qifRelevance: Reverse inference fallacy (2006). Foundational methodological constraint on brain imaging claims.

researchers.name: Michael Tennison
researchers.qifRelevance: Neuroethics and neurorights integration. Dual-use warning: security framing risks enabling surveillance.

standards.name: CVSS v4.0
standards.scope: General vulnerability scoring with extension framework
standards.qifRelevance: NISS extends CVSS v4.0 Extension Framework for neural-specific metrics

standards.name: MITRE ATT&CK
standards.qifRelevance: TARA taxonomy follows ATT&CK structure (tactics to techniques). No neural-specific coverage.

standards.name: MITRE CWE
standards.qifRelevance: No CWE entries for BCI/neural interface weaknesses. Gap identified.

standards.name: ISO 14971:2019
standards.qifRelevance: General medical device risk framework. No BCI-specific threat categories.

standards.name: IEC 62443
standards.qifRelevance: Industrial security zones/conduits model. Adaptable to BCI but no neural coverage.

standards.name: FDORA Section 524B
standards.qifRelevance: First mandatory cybersecurity requirements for medical devices including BCIs. TARA mapped to all provisions.

standards.name: EU MDR 2017/745
standards.qifRelevance: EU medical device framework. Cybersecurity mentioned but not BCI-specific.

standards.name: EU AI Act (2024/1689)
standards.scope: AI systems regulation including high-risk classification for neurotech
standards.qifRelevance: Classifies neurotechnology as high-risk AI. Requires conformity assessments.

standards.name: UNESCO Recommendation on Ethics of Neurotechnology
standards.qifRelevance: First international ethical framework for neurotechnology. Adopted at 43rd General Conference.

standards.name: OECD Recommendation on Responsible Innovation in Neurotechnology
standards.qifRelevance: First intergovernmental standard on neurotechnology governance. 9 principles.

standards.name: IEEE P2731
standards.qifRelevance: Standardized BCI/BMI terminology. Foundational for cross-framework communication.

standards.name: IEEE P2794
standards.qifRelevance: Brain data interoperability. Complements QIF data governance model.

standards.name: IEEE P7700
standards.qifRelevance: Neurotechnology-specific IEEE standard. Cabrera chairs the working group.

standards.name: Council of Europe Strategic Action Plan on Neurotechnology
standards.scope: Human rights framework for neurotechnology across 46 member states
standards.qifRelevance: Human rights-based neurotech governance for 46 countries.

legislation.name: Colorado HB 24-1058 (Neural Data as Sensitive Data)
Latin American Parliament Model Law on Neurorights
UN Human Rights Council Neurotech Mandate
EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) — Neurotech as High-Risk
Global Privacy Assembly 46th Conference Resolution on Neural Data
